meta.title: Lev Product Suite — GTM Positioning
meta.description: Product positioning, taglines, competitive analysis, and narrative structure for Lev, AgentPing, and ClawBuddy

props.title: Engine. Protocol. App.
props.subtitle: One stack, three layers, zero vendor lock-in.

props.content: Lev orchestrates AI agents. AgentPing lets humans interact with them. ClawBuddy wraps it all into a native app anyone can use. Infrastructure to protocol to product — the full stack for the agent era.

props.title: Lev — The Engine
props.content: Universal agent runtime. Orchestrates 11+ AI providers across 38+ platform adapters. Declarative YAML control plane. Fail-closed permission kernel with policy-based access control. Local-first to federated mesh.

props.title: AgentPing — The Protocol
props.content: Human-in-the-loop interaction layer. Agents emit structured intent, humans return structured actions. Exposes Lev's policy engine as time-bounded, audited permissions on every surface — web, desktop, mobile, voice, Slack, Telegram.

props.title: ClawBuddy — The App
props.content: Native AI assistant for iOS and macOS. One-click install, QR code pairing, voice chat. Local-first privacy. No terminals, no API keys, no Docker. AI that just works.

Lev is the engine — the universal agent runtime that orchestrates AI across providers, manages permissions through a fail-closed policy kernel, and runs anywhere from a Raspberry Pi to a federated mesh. It owns the policy layer: time-bounded access, audit trails, earned autonomy.

AgentPing is the protocol — the interaction layer that exposes Lev's policy engine to humans as structured, typed action surfaces. Not chat. Decisions. Approvals rendered as buttons, not messages. Permissions surfaced as toggles with expiration clocks. One protocol, every surface.

ClawBuddy is the app — the consumer product that packages the entire stack for people who will never open a terminal. One-click install spins up a local gateway invisibly. QR code pairs your phone. Voice chat works out of the box. The full power of Lev and AgentPing, wrapped in something your mom can use.

Infrastructure to protocol to product. One stack, three layers, zero vendor lock-in.

props.label: RUNTIME
props.title: Lev
props.subtitle: the open-source operating system for ai agents — one runtime, every provider, every surface.

props.content:
Lev is a universal agent runtime that orchestrates AI across 11+ providers and 38+ platform adapters. It replaces scattered prompt chains and single-vendor SDKs with a declarative control plane where agent behaviors, permissions, and workflows are auditable YAML programs — not spaghetti code.

Agents earn autonomy through demonstrated competence; permissions expire by default. The policy kernel is fail-closed: every action is time-bounded, every access is audited, every capability must be explicitly granted. Lev runs on a Raspberry Pi with no network or scales to federated mesh, local-first by design. Open-source core, enterprise governance paid — the Kubernetes model applied to AI operations.

rows.tagline: the agent runtime you'd build if you were tired of building agent runtimes
stop typing the same thing ten thousand times. orchestrate once.
the operating system for ai agents. open source. runs anywhere.
kubernetes for ai agents. open core. every provider. every surface.
ms-dos era of agentic infra. we're writing the kernel.

props.label: PROTOCOL
props.title: AgentPing
props.subtitle: the human-in-the-loop protocol for ai agents — structured actions, not just chat.

props.content:
AgentPing is an AI-native interaction protocol that replaces plain-text back-and-forth with rich, typed action surfaces. Agents emit structured intent; humans respond with structured actions — approvals, reviews, selections — not just messages.

AgentPing exposes Lev's policy engine to every surface: time-bounded permissions rendered as toggles with expiration clocks, audited actions surfaced as structured receipts, earned trust levels displayed as clear capability boundaries. One protocol renders across web, desktop, mobile, browser extensions, Slack, Telegram, and voice. GenUI is first-class: the agent generates the interface, not just the content. AgentPing is the bridge between autonomous agent work and the moments that need a human decision.

props.title: Web App
props.content: Dashboard control plane with process management, live preview, and lane-based grouping.

props.title: Desktop (Electron)
props.content: Studio environment with full agent orchestration and developer tools.

props.title: iOS / Android
props.content: Native SwiftUI and Kotlin adapters for on-the-go agent interaction.

props.title: Voice
props.content: First-class voice input/output. Speak to your agents, hear responses.

props.title: Slack / Telegram / Discord
props.content: Chat platform adapters with rich action buttons and structured responses.

props.title: Browser Extension
props.content: Chrome extension with CDP bridge for in-page agent actions.

rows.tagline: structured actions, not chat. your agent earns access, doesn't assume it.
let agents do the work. keep humans in the decisions that matter.
the protocol between ai agents and the people who use them.
surfaces lev's policy engine everywhere — time-bounded, audited, any device.
i want my agent to log into my bank for exactly 10 minutes and not one second more. agentping is how.

props.label: CONSUMER APP
props.title: ClawBuddy
props.subtitle: ai that just works — download the app, start talking.

props.content:
ClawBuddy is a native AI assistant for iOS and macOS built for people who will never touch a terminal. A one-click macOS installer spins up a local AI gateway invisibly; the iOS app connects via QR code in seconds.

Your data stays on your machine — no cloud accounts, no API keys, no Docker knowledge required. Voice chat, streaming conversations, and a curated marketplace of AI capabilities you toggle on and off like apps. ClawBuddy is what happens when infrastructure-grade AI meets consumer-grade simplicity. The full power of Lev's runtime and AgentPing's interaction protocol, wrapped in something anyone can use.

rows.tagline: local-first ai. your data never leaves your machine.
download the app, start talking. no api keys. no docker. no bullshit.
ai that just works. one click install, voice chat, runs on your mac.
consumer ai without the cloud dependency. native apps, local gateway.
chatgpt but it runs on your machine and doesn't phone home.
engine. protocol. app. the full stack for the agent era.
three layers. one stack. the infrastructure play for autonomous agents.
we're building the os jensen was talking about. from the runtime to the app store.

props.content:
we built lev because working with agents is a pain in the ass.

not the "ai is hard" kind of hard. the kind where you type the same thing ten thousand times a day. plan. spec. oh, the agent didn't capture the markdown? what a surprise. you loop, you re-prompt, you re-spec. every lifecycle state needs its own loop. we didn't want to keep typing — we wanted to think and create. so we built prompt-stack, then the loops, then the runtime that makes the loops composable. that became lev.
we didn't want to keep typing — we wanted to think and create.

agentping came from a different itch. we want our agent to log into a bank account. but we want to know it's *our* agent. we want it to have access for exactly 10 minutes. we want every action audited. we want to train it to do one very specific workflow and nothing else. that's not a chatbot problem — that's a policy problem. lev owns that policy engine: time-bounded permissions, earned trust, fail-closed by default. agentping is how that policy becomes visible — structured intent in, structured actions out, rendered on any surface. not chat. decisions.
that's not a chatbot problem — that's a policy problem.

clawbuddy is the obvious next question: if the runtime works and the protocol works, why can't anyone use it? so we wrapped the whole stack into a native app. one click, qr code, voice chat. no terminals, no api keys, no docker. your data stays on your machine.

jensen huang called this the operating system of the future, and we think he's right — we just don't know exactly what it looks like yet. nvidia built nemo and claw with the right primitives: local inference, enterprise security, policy layers. but the ux story is missing. all the little product design decisions that separate a blunt instrument from something a tastemaker would actually use. mini agent operating systems, wasm binaries, policy-layer orchestration — the pieces exist. someone needs to compose them.
we're in the ms-dos era of agentic infrastructure.

lev is betting that the os layer — runtime, protocol, and consumer surface — ships as one coherent stack, not three disconnected products from three different vendors. open source core, enterprise governance paid. runs on a raspberry pi or a fleet.

that's the bet.
